title: Hybrid Agentic AI and Multi-Agent Systems in Smart Manufacturing
author: Farahani, Mojtaba A., Khan, Md Irfan, Wuest, Thorsten
summary: This research paper proposes a hybrid agentic AI and multi-agent systems (MAS) framework for prescriptive maintenance in smart manufacturing. The approach combines LLM-based agents for strategic orchestration and high-level reasoning with specialized rule-based and smaller language model (SLM) agents for efficient, domain-specific edge tasks. The system employs a layered architecture (perception, preprocessing, analytics, optimization) coordinated by an LLM Planner Agent, supported by human-in-the-loop validation for transparency. A proof-of-concept validated on industrial datasets demonstrates automatic schema discovery, adaptive preprocessing, model optimization, and actionable maintenance recommendations with improved robustness and explainability.
content: Computer Science > Multiagent Systems arXiv:2511.18258 (cs) [Submitted on 23 Nov 2025] Title: Hybrid Agentic AI and Multi-Agent Systems in Smart Manufacturing Authors: Mojtaba A. Farahani , Md Irfan Khan , Thorsten Wuest View a PDF of the paper titled Hybrid Agentic AI and Multi-Agent Systems in Smart Manufacturing, by Mojtaba A. Farahani and 2 other authors View PDF Abstract: The convergence of Agentic AI and MAS enables a new paradigm for intelligent decision making in SMS. Traditional MAS architectures emphasize distributed coordination and specialized autonomy, while recent advances in agentic AI driven by LLMs introduce higher order reasoning, planning, and tool orchestration capabilities. This paper presents a hybrid agentic AI and multi agent framework for a Prescriptive Maintenance use case, where LLM based agents provide strategic orchestration and adaptive reasoning, complemented by rule based and SLMs agents performing efficient, domain specific tasks on the edge. The proposed framework adopts a layered architecture that consists of perception, preprocessing, analytics, and optimization layers, coordinated through an LLM Planner Agent that manages workflow decisions and context retention. Specialized agents autonomously handle schema discovery, intelligent feature analysis, model selection, and prescriptive optimization, while a HITL interface ensures transparency and auditability of generated maintenance recommendations. This hybrid design supports dynamic model adaptation, cost efficient maintenance scheduling, and interpretable decision making. An initial proof of concept implementation is validated on two industrial manufacturing datasets. The developed framework is modular and extensible, supporting seamless integration of new agents or domain modules as capabilities evolve. The results demonstrate the system capability to automatically detect schema, adapt preprocessing pipelines, optimize model performance through adaptive intelligence, and generate actionable, prioritized maintenance recommendations. The framework shows promise in achieving improved robustness, scalability, and explainability for RxM in smart manufacturing, bridging the gap between high level agentic reasoning and low level autonomous execution. takeaways: Hybrid LLM+rule-based agent design balances strategic reasoning with efficient edge execution and cost optimization.
Layered architecture with LLM Planner orchestrates perception, preprocessing, analytics, and optimization workflows with context retention.
Automatic schema discovery and adaptive preprocessing enable autonomous model selection and pipeline optimization.
Human-in-the-loop interface ensures transparency and auditability of AI-generated maintenance decisions.
Modular, extensible framework supports seamless integration of new agents and domain modules for evolving capabilities.
